title: podman run -v destination path is normalized for the wrong platform
body:
First, Thank you for this great project!

<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I have a local computer with windows and podman-remote client installed.
I have a remote server with Ubuntu 20.04 and the podman.sock enabled with root.

I wanted to try if mounting a volume works through the remote client. (this does not work with docker through ssh either)

Running the following command:

```
C:\Users\Szabi>podman run --rm -p 80:80 -v /root/temp:/usr/share/nginx/html nginx
Error: statfs /C:\root\temp: no such file or directory
```

I expected the remote server's `/root/temp` to be mounted in the container.
The error message and the docs both suggested the mounted folder is local, so I tried:

```
C:\Users\Szabi>podman run --rm -p 80:80 --mount type=bind,source=D:/temp,destination=/usr/share/nginx/html nginx
Error: invalid container path "\\usr\\share\\nginx\\html", must be an absolute path
```

to be sane, I don't expect my local folder to be mounted in the remote machine.
What would make more sense is if the mount worked as the command was executed on the remote machine.

```
root@testing-podman-droplet:~/temp# podman run --rm -p 80:80 -v /root/temp:/usr/share/nginx/html nginx
/docker-entrypoint.sh: /docker-entrypoint.d/ is not empty, will attempt to perform configuration
/docker-entrypoint.sh: Looking for shell scripts in /docker-entrypoint.d/
/docker-entrypoint.sh: Launching /docker-entrypoint.d/10-listen-on-ipv6-by-default.sh
10-listen-on-ipv6-by-default.sh: info: Getting the checksum of /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf
10-listen-on-ipv6-by-default.sh: info: Enabled listen on IPv6 in /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf
/docker-entrypoint.sh: Launching /docker-entrypoint.d/20-envsubst-on-templates.sh
/docker-entrypoint.sh: Launching /docker-entrypoint.d/30-tune-worker-processes.sh
/docker-entrypoint.sh: Configuration complete; ready for start up
2022/01/15 10:46:41 [notice] 1#1: using the "epoll" event method
2022/01/15 10:46:41 [notice] 1#1: nginx/1.21.5
2022/01/15 10:46:41 [notice] 1#1: built by gcc 10.2.1 20210110 (Debian 10.2.1-6)
2022/01/15 10:46:41 [notice] 1#1: OS: Linux 5.4.0-88-generic
2022/01/15 10:46:41 [notice] 1#1: getrlimit(RLIMIT_NOFILE): 1048576:1048576
2022/01/15 10:46:41 [notice] 1#1: start worker processes
2022/01/15 10:46:41 [notice] 1#1: start worker process 26
```

From docs:

```
--mount=type=TYPE,TYPE-SPECIFIC-OPTION[,…]

type=bind,source=D:/temp,destination=/usr/share/nginx/html
```

I think `destination=` is normalized for the wrong platform for sure.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

```
C:\Users\Szabi>podman run --rm -p 80:80 --mount type=bind,source=D:/temp,destination=/usr/share/nginx/html nginx
```

**Describe the results you received:**

```
Error: invalid container path "\\usr\\share\\nginx\\html", must be an absolute path
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

destination should be unchanged `/usr/share/nginx/html`

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
C:\Users\Szabi>podman version
Client:
Version:      3.4.4
API Version:  3.4.4
Go Version:   go1.16.8
Git Commit:   f6526ada1025c2e3f88745ba83b8b461ca659933

OS/Arch:      windows/amd64

Server:
Version:      3.4.2

Built:        Thu Jan  1 02:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

...

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Latest windows client
Latest podman on Ubuntu 20.04 on a new virtual machine

comments.body:
Podman-remote does not support mounting remote volumes, you can only use directories from the servers host.
There is some work to enable this with podman-machine in the future but the remote client itself cannot do this.

The errors looks like an actual bug, somehow the path gets converted to backslash before it is send to the server.

> to be sane, I don't expect my local folder to be mounted in the remote machine.

You would be surprised....

* #8016 

Some actually do expect this.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
We now have support for volumes on Mac and Windows platforms.  Which I believe mostly satisfies this issue.  Closing.
